---
title: "U.S. Healthcare Costs, Payments, and Operational Efficiency"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    css: styles.css
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: flatly
    navbar: []
---

```{r load_libraries}
# Libraries
library(flexdashboard)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(DT)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(purrr)
library(zoo)
library(duckdb)
library(DBI)
library(arrow)
library(stringr)
library(janitor)
```

```{r load_cms_nhe_data, include=FALSE}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Base directory for CMS NHE tables (HARD REFERENCED)
# --------------------------------------------------
nhe_dir <- "C:/Users/patterd7/Desktop/PERSONAL/PORTFOLIO/U.S. Healthcare Costs, Payments, and Operational Efficiency/nhe24-tables"

# Verify directory exists
stopifnot(dir.exists(nhe_dir))

# --------------------------------------------------
# Table 01: Aggregate & Per Capita Amounts
# --------------------------------------------------
nhe_aggregate <- readxl::read_excel(
  path = file.path(
    nhe_dir,
    "Table 01 National Health Expenditures; Aggregate and Per Capita Amounts.xlsx"
  ),
  sheet = 1
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Table 03: National Health Expenditures by Source of Funds
# (used for macro context and validation)
# --------------------------------------------------
nhe_sources <- readxl::read_excel(
  path = file.path(
    nhe_dir,
    "Table 03 National Health Expenditures, by Source of Funds.xlsx"
  ),
  sheet = 1
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Table 19: Cost Breakdown by Type of Expenditure
# --------------------------------------------------
nhe_service <- readxl::read_excel(
  path = file.path(
    nhe_dir,
    "Table 19 National Health Expenditures by Type of Expenditure and Program.xlsx"
  ),
  sheet = 1
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Validation
# --------------------------------------------------
glimpse(nhe_aggregate)
glimpse(nhe_sources)
glimpse(nhe_service)
```

```{r cleaning_table_1, include=FALSE}
# CLEANING TABLE 1 - AGGREGATE AND PER CAPITA AMOUNTS 
# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Promote first row to column names
# --------------------------------------------------
table01_clean <- nhe_aggregate %>%
  setNames(as.character(unlist(nhe_aggregate[1, ]))) %>%
  slice(-c(1, 2, 9, 11)) %>%
  rename(metric = 1)

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Keep only key metrics
# --------------------------------------------------
table01_core <- table01_clean %>%
  filter(metric %in% c(
    "National Health Expenditures",
    "U.S. Population1",
    "Gross Domestic Product2"
  ))

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: FORCE ALL YEAR COLUMNS TO CHARACTER
# (THIS FIXES YOUR ERROR)
# --------------------------------------------------
table01_core <- table01_core %>%
  mutate(across(-metric, as.character))

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Pivot into tidy long format
# --------------------------------------------------
table01_long <- table01_core %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -metric,
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year  = as.integer(year),
    value = as.numeric(value)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5: Pivot wider and safely coerce to numeric
# --------------------------------------------------
table01_tidy <- table01_long %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = metric,
    values_from = value
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    total_spending = readr::parse_number(as.character(`National Health Expenditures`)),
    population     = readr::parse_number(as.character(`U.S. Population1`)),
    gdp            = readr::parse_number(as.character(`Gross Domestic Product2`))
  ) %>%
  select(year, total_spending, population, gdp) %>%
  mutate(
    spending_per_capita = (total_spending * 1e9) / (population * 1e6),
    percent_gdp         = (total_spending / gdp) * 100,
    yoy_growth          = (total_spending / lag(total_spending)) - 1
  )
```

```{r cleaning_table_3, include=FALSE}
# --------------------------------------------------
# CLEAN TABLE 03: SOURCE OF FUNDS
# --------------------------------------------------

# STEP 1: Promote first row to column names and drop non-data rows
table03_clean <- nhe_sources %>%
  setNames(as.character(unlist(nhe_sources[1, ]))) %>%
  # Remove header + unit rows (similar logic to Table 01)
  slice(-c(1, 2)) %>%
  rename(payer = 1)

# STEP 2: Keep only major payer categories
table03_core <- table03_clean %>%
  filter(payer %in% c(
    "National Health Expenditures",
    "Private Health Insurance",
    "Medicare",
    "Medicaid",
    "Out of Pocket"
  ))

# STEP 3: Force all year columns to character (critical)
table03_core <- table03_core %>%
  mutate(across(-payer, as.character))

# STEP 4: Pivot into tidy long format
table03_long <- table03_core %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -payer,
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "spending"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year     = as.integer(year),
    spending = readr::parse_number(spending)
  )
```

```{r cleaning_table_13, include=FALSE}
# --------------------------------------------------
# TABLE 19: Cost Breakdown by Type of Expenditure
# CMS National Health Expenditures (Single Year – 2024)
# --------------------------------------------------

# STEP 1: Extract relevant rows and columns from raw CMS table
table19_raw <- nhe_service %>%
  filter(!is.na(...7)) %>%                         # Category labels live here
  rename(
    category            = ...7,
    total_spending      = ...8,
    out_of_pocket       = ...9,
    private_insurance   = ...11,
    medicare            = ...13,
    medicaid            = ...14
  ) %>%
  select(
    category,
    total_spending,
    out_of_pocket,
    private_insurance,
    medicare,
    medicaid
  )

# STEP 2: Clean category text and remove non-category rows
table19_clean <- table19_raw %>%
  mutate(category = stringr::str_trim(category)) %>%
  filter(
    !category %in% c(
      "National Health Expenditures",
      "Levels in Millions",
      "Health Consumption Expenditures",
      "Personal Health Care",
      "Professional Services",
      "Retail Outlet Sales of Medical Products",
      "Government Administration and Non-Medical Insurance",
      "Investment",
      "Research",
      "Structures",
      "Equipment"
    )
  )

# STEP 3: Force numeric safety for CMS data (handles commas, em-dashes, mixed types)
table19_numeric <- table19_clean %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      -category,
      ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.x))
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(total_spending))

# STEP 4: Map CMS internal category IDs to human-readable labels
category_lookup <- c(
  "5278588" = "Hospital Care",
  "556554"  = "Physician and Clinical Services",
  "3892868" = "Personal Health Care",
  "1644592" = "Professional Services",
  "1118000" = "Physician Services",
  "931692"  = "Clinical Services",
  "596694"  = "Other Professional Services",
  "334998"  = "Dental Services",
  "30995"   = "Other Health, Residential, and Personal Care",
  "22118"   = "Home Health Care",
  "8877"    = "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities",
  "50009"   = "Retail Prescription Drugs",
  "117580"  = "Durable Medical Equipment",
  "432933"  = "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
)

table19_final <- table19_numeric %>%
  mutate(
    category = dplyr::recode(category, !!!category_lookup)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(category)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_spending))

# STEP 5: Compute payer shares (for efficiency & dependency analysis)
table19_final <- table19_final %>%
  mutate(
    private_share   = private_insurance / total_spending,
    medicare_share  = medicare / total_spending,
    medicaid_share  = medicaid / total_spending,
    oop_share       = out_of_pocket / total_spending
  )

table19_final <- table19_final %>%
  filter(category %in% c(
    "Hospital Care",
    "Physician and Clinical Services",
    "Retail Prescription Drugs",
    "Home Health Care",
    "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities",
    "Dental Services",
    "Durable Medical Equipment",
    "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
  ))

# FINAL VALIDATION
glimpse(table19_final)
```

```{r summary_metrics, include=FALSE}
# ---- Latest year snapshot ----
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)

summary_snapshot <- table01_tidy %>%
  dplyr::filter(year == latest_year)

# ---- Optional payer snapshot ----
if (exists("table03_tidy")) {
  payer_snapshot <- table03_tidy %>%
    dplyr::filter(year == latest_year)

  public_share <- (payer_snapshot$medicare_share +
                   payer_snapshot$medicaid_share) * 100
} else {
  public_share <- NA_real_
}
```


# Summary Page

<!-- VALUE BOX STYLE -->

```{=html}
<style>
.value-box {
  padding: 10px !important;
  font-size: 13px !important;
  min-height: 80px !important;
}
.value-box .value {
  font-size: 26px !important;
  font-weight: 700 !important;
  line-height: 1.1 !important;
}
.value-box .caption {
  font-size: 12px !important;
  font-weight: 600 !important;
}

</style>
```

## Row {data-height="75"}

### Column {width="20%"}

```{r}
# ValueBox - Total U.s. Healthcare Spending
flexdashboard::valueBox(
  value = paste0(
    "$",
    round(summary_snapshot$total_spending / 1000, 2),
    "T"
  ),
  caption = paste(
    "Total U.S. Healthcare Spending (",
    latest_year,
    ")",
    sep = ""
  ),
  icon = "fa-dollar-sign",
  color = "primary"
)
```

### Column {width="20%"}

```{r}
# Value Box - Healthcare share of GDP 
flexdashboard::valueBox(
  value = paste0(round(summary_snapshot$percent_gdp, 1), "%"),
  caption = "Healthcare Share of U.S. GDP (2024)",
  icon = "fa-chart-pie",
  color = "warning"
)
```

### Column {width="20%"}
```{r}
# Value Box - Healthcare Spending Per Person
flexdashboard::valueBox(
  value = paste0(
    "$",
    scales::comma(round(summary_snapshot$spending_per_capita, 0))
  ),
  caption = "Healthcare Spending per Person (2024)",
  icon = "fa-user",
  color = "info"
)
```
### Column {width="20%"}
```{r}
# Value Box - YOY spending growth 
flexdashboard::valueBox(
  value = paste0(round(summary_snapshot$yoy_growth * 100, 1), "%"),
  caption = "Year‑over‑Year Spending Growth (2024)",
  icon = "fa-arrow-trend-up",
  color = "danger"
)
```
### Column {width="20%"}
```{r}
flexdashboard::valueBox(
  value = {
    public_share_calc <- sum(table19_final$medicare + table19_final$medicaid, na.rm = TRUE) /
                          sum(table19_final$total_spending, na.rm = TRUE)

    paste0(round(public_share_calc * 100, 0), "% Public")
  },
  caption = "Medicare + Medicaid Share of Total Spending",
  icon = "fa-building-columns",
  color = "success"
)

```

## Row {.tabset}
-------------------------------------

### Healthcare’s Share of the U.S. Economy

```{r healthcare_spending_trillions_final}

library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)

# --------------------------------------------------
# CPI table 
# --------------------------------------------------
cpi_tbl <- tribble(
~year, ~cpi_index,
1960L, 29.6, 1970L, 38.8, 1980L, 82.4, 1990L, 130.7,
2000L, 172.2, 2005L, 195.3, 2010L, 218.1, 2015L, 237.0,
2020L, 258.8, 2021L, 270.9, 2022L, 292.7,
2023L, 305.3, 2024L, 315.0
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Data prep — Total spending in trillions (nominal + real)
# --------------------------------------------------
health_trillions_ts <- table01_tidy %>%
left_join(cpi_tbl, by = "year") %>%
arrange(year) %>%
fill(cpi_index, .direction = "downup") %>%
mutate(
date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01")),
spending_nominal = total_spending / 1000, # trillions
spending_real = spending_nominal * (last(cpi_index) / cpi_index)
)

# Hover text
hover_nominal <- paste0(
"<b>Year:</b> ", health_trillions_ts$year, "<br>",
"<b>Spending:</b> $", sprintf("%.2f", health_trillions_ts$spending_nominal), "T (nominal)"
)

hover_real <- paste0(
"<b>Year:</b> ", health_trillions_ts$year, "<br>",
"<b>Spending:</b> $", sprintf("%.2f", health_trillions_ts$spending_real), "T (inflation-adjusted)"
)

# Clamp x-axis
xmin <- min(health_trillions_ts$date)
xmax <- max(health_trillions_ts$date)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot — Exact visual match to per-person chart
# --------------------------------------------------
p <- plot_ly() %>%
add_trace(
x = health_trillions_ts$date,
y = round(health_trillions_ts$spending_nominal, 2),
type = "scatter",
mode = "lines",
line = list(color = "#16a34a", width = 3.4),
hovertext = hover_nominal,
hoverinfo = "text"
) %>%
layout(

title = list(
text = paste0(
"U.S. Healthcare Spending Over Time<br>",
"<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
"Total national health expenditures (in trillions USD)"
),
x = 0.5,
xanchor = "center",
y = 0.94,
font = list(size = 21, color = "#111827")
),

# Annotations 
annotations = list(

# Medicare & Medicaid (1965)
list(
x = as.Date("1965-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>1965</b><br>Medicare & Medicaid",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 11, color = "#1e40af")
),

# Affordable Care Act (2010)
list(
x = as.Date("2010-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>2010</b><br>Affordable Care Act",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 11, color = "#92400e")
),

# COVID 19 (2020)
list(
x = as.Date("2020-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>COVID 19</b><br>System shock",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 12, color = "#991b1b")
),

# How-to-read guide 
list(
x = as.Date("1991-06-01"),
y = 0.84,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
xanchor = "center",
align = "center",
showarrow = FALSE,
text =
"<b>How to read this chart</b><br>
<span style='color:#374151; line-height:1.35;'>
• <b>Line</b>: total U.S. healthcare spending<br>
• <b>Buttons</b>: toggle nominal vs inflation adjusted dollars<br>
• <b>Shaded bands</b>: major system changes<br>
• <b>Key takeaway</b>: costs rise over time regardless of inflation
</span>",
font = list(size = 11),
bgcolor = "rgba(255,255,255,0.92)",
bordercolor = "#9ca3af",
borderwidth = 2,
borderpad = 8
)
),

# Axes
xaxis = list(
title = list(text = "Year", font = list(size = 14), standoff = 22),
tickformat = "%Y",
tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
range = c(xmin, xmax),
rangeselector = list(
buttons = list(
list(count = 5, label = "5Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(count = 20, label = "20Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(step = "all", label = "All Time")
),
bgcolor = "#f3f4f6",
font = list(color = "#111827")
),
rangeslider = list(
visible = TRUE,
thickness = 0.065,
bgcolor = "#e5e7eb",
bordercolor = "#e5e7eb"
)
),

yaxis = list(
title = list(text = "Spending (Trillions USD)", font = list(size = 14)),
tickprefix = "$",
ticksuffix = "T",
tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
range = c(0, 8), 
gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
),

# Buttons 
updatemenus = list(
list(
type = "buttons",
direction = "right",
x = 0.5,
y = 0.93,
xanchor = "center",
yanchor = "top",
buttons = list(
list(
label = "Nominal",
method = "restyle",
args = list(
list(
y = list(round(health_trillions_ts$spending_nominal, 2)),
hovertext = list(hover_nominal),
line = list(color = "#16a34a")
)
)
),
list(
label = "Inflation-adjusted",
method = "restyle",
args = list(
list(
y = list(round(health_trillions_ts$spending_real, 2)),
hovertext = list(hover_real),
line = list(color = "#2563eb")
)
)
)
)
)
),

# Shaded bands
shapes = list(
list(type="rect", x0="1965-01-01", x1="1966-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(59,130,246,0.07)", line=list(width=0)),
list(type="rect", x0="2010-01-01", x1="2011-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(245,158,11,0.09)", line=list(width=0)),
list(type="rect", x0="2020-01-01", x1="2021-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(220,38,38,0.11)", line=list(width=0))
),

hovermode = "x unified",
margin = list(l = 68, r = 40, t = 120, b = 90)
) %>%
config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p

```

### U.S. healthcare spending per person over time

```{r healthcare_spending_per_person_final}

library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(tibble)

# --------------------------------------------------
# CPI table
# --------------------------------------------------
cpi_tbl <- tribble(
~year, ~cpi_index,
1960L, 29.6, 1970L, 38.8, 1980L, 82.4, 1990L, 130.7,
2000L, 172.2, 2005L, 195.3, 2010L, 218.1, 2015L, 237.0,
2020L, 258.8, 2021L, 270.9, 2022L, 292.7,
2023L, 305.3, 2024L, 315.0
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Data prep — Per person spending (nominal + real)
# --------------------------------------------------
health_pc_ts <- table01_tidy %>%
left_join(cpi_tbl, by = "year") %>%
arrange(year) %>%
fill(cpi_index, .direction = "downup") %>%
mutate(
date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01")),
spending_nominal = (total_spending / population) * 1000,
spending_real = spending_nominal * (last(cpi_index) / cpi_index)
)

# Hover text
hover_nominal <- paste0(
"<b>Year:</b> ", health_pc_ts$year, "<br>",
"<b>Cost per person:</b> ",
dollar(health_pc_ts$spending_nominal, accuracy = 1),
" (nominal)"
)

hover_real <- paste0(
"<b>Year:</b> ", health_pc_ts$year, "<br>",
"<b>Cost per person:</b> ",
dollar(health_pc_ts$spending_real, accuracy = 1),
" (inflation-adjusted)"
)

# Clamp x-axis
xmin <- min(health_pc_ts$date)
xmax <- max(health_pc_ts$date)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot — Exact visual replication of your original per-person chart
# --------------------------------------------------
p <- plot_ly() %>%
add_trace(
x = health_pc_ts$date,
y = round(health_pc_ts$spending_nominal, 0),
type = "scatter",
mode = "lines",
line = list(color = "#16a34a", width = 3.4),
hovertext = hover_nominal,
hoverinfo = "text"
) %>%
layout(

title = list(
text = paste0(
"U.S. Healthcare Spending per Person<br>",
"<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
"Total national health expenditures divided by U.S. population"
),
x = 0.5,
xanchor = "center",
y = 0.94,
font = list(size = 21, color = "#111827")
),

# Annotations 
annotations = list(

list(
x = as.Date("1965-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>1965</b><br>Medicare & Medicaid",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 11, color = "#1e40af")
),

list(
x = as.Date("2010-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>2010</b><br>Affordable Care Act",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 11, color = "#92400e")
),

list(
x = as.Date("2020-07-01"),
y = 0.95,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
text = "<b>COVID 19</b><br>System shock",
showarrow = FALSE,
font = list(size = 12, color = "#991b1b")
),

# How-to-read 
list(
x = as.Date("1991-06-01"),
y = 0.84,
xref = "x",
yref = "paper",
xanchor = "center",
align = "center",
showarrow = FALSE,
text =
"<b>How to read this chart</b><br>
<span style='color:#374151; line-height:1.35;'>
• <b>Line</b>: U.S. healthcare spending per person<br>
• <b>Buttons</b>: toggle nominal vs inflation adjusted dollars<br>
• <b>Shaded bands</b>: major system changes<br>
• <b>Key takeaway</b>: costs rise over time regardless of inflation
</span>",
font = list(size = 11),
bgcolor = "rgba(255,255,255,0.92)",
bordercolor = "#9ca3af",
borderwidth = 2,
borderpad = 8
)
),

# Axes
xaxis = list(
title = list(text = "Year", font = list(size = 14), standoff = 22),
tickformat = "%Y",
tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
range = c(xmin, xmax),
rangeselector = list(
buttons = list(
list(count = 5, label = "5Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(count = 20, label = "20Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
list(step = "all", label = "All Time")
),
bgcolor = "#f3f4f6",
font = list(color = "#111827")
),
rangeslider = list(
visible = TRUE,
thickness = 0.065,
bgcolor = "#e5e7eb",
bordercolor = "#e5e7eb"
)
),

yaxis = list(
title = list(text = "Annual cost per person (USD)", font = list(size = 14)),
tickprefix = "$",
tickformat = "~s",
tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
range = c(0, 20000),
gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
),

# Buttons 
updatemenus = list(
list(
type = "buttons",
direction = "right",
x = 0.5,
y = 0.93,
xanchor = "center",
yanchor = "top",
buttons = list(
list(
label = "Nominal",
method = "restyle",
args = list(
list(
y = list(round(health_pc_ts$spending_nominal, 0)),
hovertext = list(hover_nominal),
line = list(color = "#16a34a")
)
)
),
list(
label = "Inflation-adjusted",
method = "restyle",
args = list(
list(
y = list(round(health_pc_ts$spending_real, 0)),
hovertext = list(hover_real),
line = list(color = "#2563eb")
)
)
)
)
)
),

# Shaded bands
shapes = list(
list(type="rect", x0="1965-01-01", x1="1966-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(59,130,246,0.07)", line=list(width=0)),
list(type="rect", x0="2010-01-01", x1="2011-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(245,158,11,0.09)", line=list(width=0)),
list(type="rect", x0="2020-01-01", x1="2021-01-01",
y0=0, y1=1, xref="x", yref="paper",
fillcolor="rgba(220,38,38,0.11)", line=list(width=0))
),

hovermode = "x unified",
margin = list(l = 68, r = 40, t = 120, b = 90)
) %>%
config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p



```

### Year Over Year Spending Growth 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Data prep: true YoY, last 25 years
# --------------------------------------------------
yoy_ts <- table01_tidy %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  mutate(
    year_gap = year - lag(year),
    yoy_pct  = (total_spending / lag(total_spending) - 1) * 100
  ) %>%
  filter(year_gap == 1) %>%
  filter(year >= max(year, na.rm = TRUE) - 25) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01")))

xmin <- min(yoy_ts$date)
xmax <- max(yoy_ts$date)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p <- plot_ly(yoy_ts) %>%
  add_bars(
    x = ~date,
    y = ~round(yoy_pct, 1),
    marker = list(
      color = "coral",   
      line  = list(color = "black", width = 0.5)
    ),
    hovertext = ~paste0(
      "<b>Year:</b> ", year, "<br>",
      "<b>Growth:</b> ", round(yoy_pct, 1), "%"
    ),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%

  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Year‑over‑Year Growth in U.S. Healthcare Spending<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Annual percent change in national health expenditures (last 25 years)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      font = list(size = 21, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = list(text = "Year", font = list(size = 14), standoff = 22),
      tickformat = "%Y",
      range = c(xmin, xmax),
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),

      rangeselector = list(
        buttons = list(
          list(count = 5,  label = "5Y",  step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 25, label = "25Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward")
        ),
        bgcolor = "#f3f4f6",
        font = list(color = "#111827")
      ),

      rangeslider = list(
        visible = TRUE,
        thickness = 0.055,
        bgcolor = "#e5e7eb",
        bordercolor = "#e5e7eb"
      ),

      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = list(text = "Annual growth rate (%)", font = list(size = 14)),
      ticksuffix = "%",
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
    ),

    # --------------------------------------------------
    # Context shading (subtle, behind bars)
    # --------------------------------------------------
    shapes = list(

      # Affordable Care Act (2010)
      list(
        type = "rect",
        x0 = as.Date("2010-01-01"),
        x1 = as.Date("2011-01-01"),
        y0 = 0, y1 = 1,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        fillcolor = "rgba(245,158,11,0.10)",
        line = list(width = 0),
        layer = "below"
      ),

      # COVID-19 (2020)
      list(
        type = "rect",
        x0 = as.Date("2020-01-01"),
        x1 = as.Date("2021-01-01"),
        y0 = 0, y1 = 1,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        fillcolor = "rgba(220,38,38,0.12)",
        line = list(width = 0),
        layer = "below"
      ),

      # Zero line
      list(
        type = "line",
        x0 = xmin,
        x1 = xmax,
        y0 = 0,
        y1 = 0,
        line = list(color = "#9ca3af", width = 1, dash = "dot")
      )
    ),

    annotations = list(
      list(
        x = as.Date("2010-07-01"),
        y = 1.05,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        text = "<b>2010</b><br>Affordable Care Act",
        showarrow = FALSE,
        font = list(size = 11, color = "#92400e")
      ),
      list(
        x = as.Date("2020-07-01"),
        y = 1.05,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        text = "<b>2020</b><br>COVID‑19",
        showarrow = FALSE,
        font = list(size = 12, color = "#991b1b")
      )
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",
    margin = list(l = 68, r = 40, t = 120, b = 90),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif", size = 12)
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p

```


### Medicare and Medicaid Share of Total Spending 

```{r public_share_bar_GREEN_V3_FINAL, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Public share of spending (Medicare + Medicaid)
# Last 25 years only + dynamic y-axis range
# --------------------------------------------------
public_share_ts <- table03_long %>%
  filter(payer %in% c(
    "National Health Expenditures",
    "Medicare",
    "Medicaid"
  )) %>%
  group_by(year, payer) %>%
  summarise(
    spending = sum(as.numeric(spending), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = payer,
    values_from = spending
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Medicare),
    !is.na(Medicaid),
    !is.na(`National Health Expenditures`)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    public_share_pct =
      100 * (Medicare + Medicaid) / `National Health Expenditures`
  ) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  filter(year >= max(year, na.rm = TRUE) - 25) %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01"))
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Dynamic y-axis range (padded, rounded)
# --------------------------------------------------
y_min <- floor(min(public_share_ts$public_share_pct) / 5) * 5
y_max <- ceiling(max(public_share_ts$public_share_pct) / 5) * 5

xmin <- min(public_share_ts$date)
xmax <- max(public_share_ts$date)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p <- plot_ly(public_share_ts) %>%
  add_bars(
    x = ~date,
    y = ~round(public_share_pct, 1),
    marker = list(
      color = "rgba(22,163,74,0.85)",   # muted green
      line  = list(color = "black", width = 0.5)
    ),
    hovertext = ~paste0(
      "<b>Year:</b> ", year, "<br>",
      "<b>Medicare + Medicaid:</b> ",
      round(public_share_pct, 1), "%"
    ),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%

  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Medicare and Medicaid Share of U.S. Healthcare Spending<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Percent of total national health expenditures",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      font = list(size = 21, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = list(text = "Year", font = list(size = 14), standoff = 22),
      tickformat = "%Y",
      range = c(xmin, xmax),
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),

      rangeselector = list(
        buttons = list(
          list(count = 5,  label = "5Y",  step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 25, label = "25Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward")
        ),
        bgcolor = "#f3f4f6",
        font = list(color = "#111827")
      ),

      rangeslider = list(
        visible = TRUE,
        thickness = 0.055,
        bgcolor = "#e5e7eb",
        bordercolor = "#e5e7eb"
      ),

      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = list(
        text = "Public share of total spending (%)",
        font = list(size = 14)
      ),
      range = c(y_min, y_max),         # dynamic, accurate range
      ticksuffix = "%",
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
    ),

    # --------------------------------------------------
    # Context shading
    # --------------------------------------------------
    shapes = list(

      list(
        type = "rect",
        x0 = as.Date("2010-01-01"),
        x1 = as.Date("2011-01-01"),
        y0 = 0, y1 = 1,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        fillcolor = "rgba(245,158,11,0.10)",
        line = list(width = 0),
        layer = "below"
      ),

      list(
        type = "rect",
        x0 = as.Date("2020-01-01"),
        x1 = as.Date("2021-01-01"),
        y0 = 0, y1 = 1,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        fillcolor = "rgba(220,38,38,0.12)",
        line = list(width = 0),
        layer = "below"
      )
    ),

    annotations = list(
      list(
        x = as.Date("2010-07-01"),
        y = 1.05,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        text = "<b>2010</b><br>Affordable Care Act",
        showarrow = FALSE,
        font = list(size = 11, color = "#92400e")
      ),
      list(
        x = as.Date("2020-07-01"),
        y = 1.05,
        xref = "x", yref = "paper",
        text = "<b>2020</b><br>COVID‑19",
        showarrow = FALSE,
        font = list(size = 12, color = "#991b1b")
      )
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",
    margin = list(l = 68, r = 40, t = 120, b = 90),
    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif", size = 12)
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p
```

### Spending Drivers

```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Cost drivers — FULL SHARE OF TOTAL NATIONAL SPENDING (100%)
# --------------------------------------------------

# Use the SAME total NHE as the headline ($5.28T)
# Assumes table01_tidy$total_spending is in BILLIONS
total_nhe_trillions <- table01_tidy %>%
  arrange(desc(year)) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  pull(total_spending) / 1000


# Base service-level drivers
cost_drivers_core <- table19_final %>%
  select(category, total_spending) %>%
  mutate(
    # CMS Table 19 is in MILLIONS
    spending_trillions = total_spending / 1e6,
    share_pct = 100 * spending_trillions / total_nhe_trillions,

    category_short = case_when(
      category == "Hospital Care" ~ "Hospitals",
      category == "Physician and Clinical Services" ~ "Physicians",
      category == "Retail Prescription Drugs" ~ "Rx Drugs",
      category == "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" ~ "Nursing Care",
      category == "Home Health Care" ~ "Home Health",
      category == "Dental Services" ~ "Dental",
      category == "Durable Medical Equipment" ~ "DME",
      category == "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" ~ "Other Medical",
      TRUE ~ category
    )
  )


# --------------------------------------------------
# Add "All Other Spending" to force 100%
# --------------------------------------------------
other_share_pct <- 100 - sum(cost_drivers_core$share_pct, na.rm = TRUE)

cost_drivers <- bind_rows(
  cost_drivers_core %>%
    select(category, category_short, spending_trillions, share_pct),

  tibble(
    category = "All Other Health Spending",
    category_short = "
    (Admin, Public Health, Insurance, Investment)",
    spending_trillions = total_nhe_trillions * other_share_pct / 100,
    share_pct = other_share_pct
  )
) %>%
  arrange(desc(share_pct)) %>%
  mutate(category_short = factor(category_short, levels = category_short))


# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p <- plot_ly(cost_drivers) %>%
  add_bars(
    x = ~category_short,
    y = ~share_pct,
    marker = list(
      color = "rgba(59,130,246,0.80)",
      line  = list(color = "#1e40af", width = 0.6)
    ),
    hovertext = ~paste0(
      "<b>Category:</b> ", category, "<br>",
      "<b>Share of total spending:</b> ", round(share_pct, 1), "%<br>",
      "<b>Annual spending:</b> $", round(spending_trillions, 2), "T"
    ),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "What Drives U.S. Healthcare Costs?<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Full distribution of total national health expenditures (latest year)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      font = list(size = 21, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "",
      tickangle = 0,
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = list(text = "Share of total spending (%)", font = list(size = 14)),
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range = c(0, max(cost_drivers$share_pct) * 1.2),
      tickfont = list(color = "#374151"),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.08)"
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",
    margin = list(l = 80, r = 40, t = 120, b = 110),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    ),

    annotations = lapply(seq_len(nrow(cost_drivers)), function(i) {
      list(
        x = cost_drivers$category_short[i],
        y = cost_drivers$share_pct[i] + 1,
        text = paste0(round(cost_drivers$share_pct[i], 1), "%"),
        showarrow = FALSE,
        font = list(size = 12, color = "#111827"),
        xanchor = "center",
        yanchor = "bottom"
      )
    })
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p
```


# Where the Money Goes (Deep Dive)

## Row {.tabset}

### Category Trend Over Time 

```{r table02_category_trends}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Load raw Table 02
# --------------------------------------------------
table02_path <- "C:/Users/patterd7/Desktop/PERSONAL/PORTFOLIO/U.S. Healthcare Costs, Payments, and Operational Efficiency/nhe24-tables/Table 02 National Health Expenditures, Aggregate and Per Capita Amounts, by Type of Expenditure.xlsx"

raw_tbl <- read_excel(
  table02_path,
  sheet = 1,
  col_names = FALSE
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Identify the LEVELS section (billions of dollars)
# Starts at "National Health Expenditures"
# Ends at "Structures and Equipment"
# --------------------------------------------------
levels_tbl <- raw_tbl %>%
  rename(category = ...1) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(category),
    category %in% c(
      "National Health Expenditures",
      "Health Consumption Expenditures",
      "Personal Health Care",
      "Hospital Care",
      "Professional Services",
      "Physician and Clinical Services",
      "Prescription Drugs",
      "Dental Services",
      "Home Health Care",
      "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities",
      "Retail Outlets Sales of Medical Products",
      "Durable Medical Equipment",
      "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
    )
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Manually define year vector (matches column order)
# --------------------------------------------------
years <- c(
  1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2005, 2010,
  2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021,
  2022, 2023, 2024
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Select numeric columns ONLY and reshape
# --------------------------------------------------
category_ts <- levels_tbl %>%
  select(category, where(is.numeric)) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -category,
    values_to = "spending",
    names_to = "col_index"
  ) %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  mutate(year = years[row_number()]) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    spending = spending / 1000   # billions → trillions
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year))
```

```{r category_trend_plot_clean}
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)

# Ensure ordering + date column
category_ts <- category_ts %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01")),
    category = factor(
      category,
      levels = rev(c(
        "Hospital Care",
        "Physician and Clinical Services",
        "Prescription Drugs",
        "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities",
        "Home Health Care",
        "Dental Services",
        "Durable Medical Equipment",
        "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
      ))
    )
  )

# Color palette (unchanged)
palette <- c(
  "Hospital Care" = "#0f766e",
  "Physician and Clinical Services" = "#0284c7",
  "Prescription Drugs" = "#f59e0b",
  "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "#a855f7",
  "Home Health Care" = "#10b981",
  "Dental Services" = "#fca5a5",           
  "Durable Medical Equipment" = "#ef4444", 
  "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "#94a3b8"
)

# -------------------------------
# Plot
# -------------------------------
p <- plot_ly(
  data  = category_ts,
  x     = ~date,
  y     = ~spending,
  color = ~category,
  colors = palette,
  type  = "scatter",
  mode  = "none",
  fill  = "tonexty",
  hovertemplate = paste(
  "<b>%{fullData.name}</b><br>",
  "Spending: <b>$%{y:.2f}T</b>",
  "<extra></extra>"
)
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Where U.S. Healthcare Dollars Go Over Time<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "National health expenditures by service category (nominal dollars)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,   # prevents clipping
      font = list(size = 22, color = "#111827")
    ),

    # Capitalized axis titles
    xaxis = list(
      title = "Year",
      tickformat = "%Y",
      showgrid = FALSE,
      rangeselector = list(
        buttons = list(
          list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 20, label = "20Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(step = "all", label = "All Time")
        )
      ),
      rangeslider = list(
        visible = TRUE,
        thickness = 0.05,
        bgcolor = "#e5e7eb"
      )
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Spending (Trillions USD, Selected Categories)", # clarified
      tickprefix = "$",
      ticksuffix = "T",
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    legend = list(
      title = list(text = ""),
      orientation = "v",
      x = 1.02,
      y = 1,
      font = list(size = 11)
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",

    # Extra top space for the title
    margin = list(
      l = 70,
      r = 170,
      t = 140,
      b = 80
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(
    displaylogo = FALSE,
    modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d")
  )

p

```

### Category Share Over Time 

```{r category_share_prep}
library(dplyr)

category_share_ts <- category_ts %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(
    selected_total = sum(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_pct = 100 * spending / selected_total
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01"))
  )
```

```{r category_share_plot}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Re-base shares CORRECTLY
# (within selected categories, by YEAR)
# --------------------------------------------------
category_share_ts <- category_ts %>%
  filter(category %in% c(
    "Hospital Care",
    "Physician and Clinical Services",
    "Prescription Drugs",
    "Dental Services",
    "Durable Medical Equipment",
    "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
  )) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(
    selected_total = sum(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_pct = 100 * spending / selected_total
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01")),
    category = factor(
      category,
      levels = c(
        "Hospital Care",
        "Physician and Clinical Services",
        "Prescription Drugs",
        "Dental Services",
        "Durable Medical Equipment",
        "Other Non-Durable Medical Products"
      )
    )
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_share <- plot_ly(
  data  = category_share_ts,
  x     = ~date,
  y     = ~share_pct,
  color = ~category,
  colors = palette,
  type  = "scatter",
  mode  = "lines",
  line  = list(width = 3),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{fullData.name}</b><br>",
    "Share of spending: <b>%{y:.1f}%</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "How the Composition of U.S. Healthcare Spending Has Changed<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Share of spending within major healthcare service categories",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Year",
      tickformat = "%Y",
      showgrid = FALSE,
      rangeselector = list(
        buttons = list(
          list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 20, label = "20Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(step = "all", label = "All Time")
        )
      ),
      rangeslider = list(
        visible = TRUE,
        thickness = 0.05,
        bgcolor = "#e5e7eb"
      )
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Share of Spending (%)",
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range = c(0, 65),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    legend = list(
      title = list(text = ""),
      orientation = "v",
      x = 1.02,
      y = 1,
      font = list(size = 11),
      traceorder = "normal"   
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",

    margin = list(
      l = 70,
      r = 170,
      t = 140,
      b = 80
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(
    displaylogo = FALSE,
    modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d")
  )

p_share
```


# Who Pays for What

## Row {.tabset}

### Stacked Bars by Category (Medicare, Medicaid, Private Insurance, and Out of Pocket)

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Build payer-by-category table (latest year)
# --------------------------------------------------
payer_by_category <- table19_final %>%
  select(
    category,
    medicare,
    medicaid,
    private_insurance,
    out_of_pocket
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -category,
    names_to = "payer",
    values_to = "spending"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Table 19 is in MILLIONS → convert to BILLIONS
    spending = spending / 1000,

    payer = recode(
      payer,
      medicare = "Medicare",
      medicaid = "Medicaid",
      private_insurance = "Private Insurance",
      out_of_pocket = "Out of Pocket"
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------------------
# Order categories by TOTAL spending
# ----------------------------------------
category_order <- payer_by_category %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(total_spending = sum(spending, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_spending)) %>%
  pull(category)

# ----------------------------------------
# Short, readable category labels
# ----------------------------------------
category_labels <- c(
  "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
  "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
  "Retail Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
  "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
  "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
  "Dental Services" = "Dental",
  "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
  "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
)

payer_by_category_clean <- payer_by_category %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category_order),
    category_short = recode(category, !!!category_labels)
  )

library(plotly)

payer_palette <- c(
  "Medicare" = "#2563eb",
  "Medicaid" = "#22c55e",
  "Private Insurance" = "#f59e0b",
  "Out of Pocket" = "#ef4444"
)

p_payers <- plot_ly(
  data  = payer_by_category_clean,
  x     = ~category_short,
  y     = ~spending,
  color = ~payer,
  colors = payer_palette,
  type  = "bar",
  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "<b>%{fullData.name}</b><br>",
    "Spending: <b>$%{y:.1f}B</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    barmode = "stack",

    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Who Pays for U.S. Healthcare Services?<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Spending by payer and service category (latest year)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Service Category",
      showgrid = FALSE,
      tickangle = 0
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Spending (Billions USD)",
      tickprefix = "$",
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Payer"),
      orientation = "v",
      x = 1.05,
      y = 1,
      font = list(size = 11),
      traceorder = "reversed"
    ),

    margin = list(
      l = 80,
      r = 180,
      t = 140,
      b = 90
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(
    displaylogo = FALSE,
    modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d")
  )

p_payers

```

### Private Insurance vs Public (Medicare and Medicaid)

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Prepare Public vs Private SHARE data
# --------------------------------------------------
public_private_share <- table19_final %>%
  mutate(
    Public  = medicare + medicaid,
    Private = private_insurance + out_of_pocket
  ) %>%
  select(category, Public, Private) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Public, Private),
    names_to = "payer_group",
    values_to = "spending"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    spending = spending / 1000   # millions -> billions
  ) %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  mutate(
    total_spending = sum(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    share_pct = 100 * spending / total_spending
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# --------------------------------------------------
# Short labels + ORDER BY PUBLIC SHARE (descending)
# --------------------------------------------------
category_labels <- c(
  "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
  "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
  "Retail Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
  "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
  "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
  "Dental Services" = "Dental",
  "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
  "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
)

category_order <- public_private_share %>%
  filter(payer_group == "Public") %>%
  arrange(desc(share_pct)) %>%
  pull(category)

public_private_share <- public_private_share %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category_order),
    category_short = recode(category, !!!category_labels)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Build connector lines (one per category)
# --------------------------------------------------
connectors <- public_private_share %>%
  select(category_short, payer_group, share_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = payer_group, values_from = share_pct)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot: TRUE dumbbell chart
# --------------------------------------------------
p_public_private <- plot_ly() %>%

  # Connector lines
  add_segments(
    data = connectors,
    x = ~Public,
    xend = ~Private,
    y = ~category_short,
    yend = ~category_short,
    line = list(color = "rgba(0,0,0,0.25)", width = 2),
    hoverinfo = "skip",
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) %>%

  # Public dots
  add_markers(
    data = public_private_share %>% filter(payer_group == "Public"),
    x = ~share_pct,
    y = ~category_short,
    marker = list(size = 12, color = "#2563eb"),
    name = "Public",
    hovertemplate = paste(
      "<b>%{y}</b><br>",
      "Public share: <b>%{x:.1f}%</b>",
      "<extra></extra>"
    )
  ) %>%

  # Private dots
  add_markers(
    data = public_private_share %>% filter(payer_group == "Private"),
    x = ~share_pct,
    y = ~category_short,
    marker = list(size = 12, color = "#f59e0b"),
    name = "Private",
    hovertemplate = paste(
      "<b>%{y}</b><br>",
      "Private share: <b>%{x:.1f}%</b>",
      "<extra></extra>"
    )
  ) %>%

  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Public vs Private Payment by Healthcare Service<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Share of spending funded by public programs versus private payers (latest year)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Share of Spending (%)",
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range = c(0, 100),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "",
      autorange = "reversed"
    ),

    legend = list(
      orientation = "h",
      x = 0.5,
      y = -0.25,
      xanchor = "center"
    ),

    margin = list(
      l = 150,
      r = 60,
      t = 140,
      b = 120
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(
    displaylogo = FALSE,
    modeBarButtonsToRemove = c("lasso2d", "select2d")
  )

p_public_private
```


# Cost Pressure and Growth Mechanics

## Row {.tabset}

### Total Spending Growth 

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Assumes table01_tidy and category_ts already exist
# --------------------------------------------------

# --------------------------------------------------
# Total spending growth (10-year window)
# --------------------------------------------------
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)
start_year  <- latest_year - 10

total_growth_contrib <- table01_tidy %>%
  filter(year %in% c(start_year, latest_year)) %>%
  summarise(
    total_growth_pct =
      (last(total_spending) / first(total_spending) - 1),
    base_spending =
      first(total_spending)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    total_growth_spending = base_spending * total_growth_pct
  ) %>%
  pull(total_growth_spending)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Recent category spending shares
# --------------------------------------------------
category_shares <- category_ts %>%
  filter(year >= latest_year - 3) %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_spending = mean(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share = avg_spending / sum(avg_spending)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Allocate total growth across categories
# --------------------------------------------------
total_growth_by_category <- category_shares %>%
  mutate(
    growth_spending_trillions = share * total_growth_contrib / 1000
  ) %>%
  arrange(growth_spending_trillions) %>%   # (gradient logic)
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category),
    category_short = recode(
      category,
      "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
      "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
      "Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
      "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
      "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
      "Dental Services" = "Dental",
      "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
      "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
    ),
    label = paste0("$", sprintf("%.2f", growth_spending_trillions), "T")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Color gradient (lighter green → darker green)
# --------------------------------------------------
total_gradient <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#9ae6c1", "#059669")
)(
  nrow(total_growth_by_category)
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Standardized y-axis max (matches other charts)
# --------------------------------------------------
y_actual_max      <- max(
  total_growth_by_category$growth_spending_trillions,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

y_axis_reference  <- 3.0   
y_max_fixed       <- max(y_actual_max, y_axis_reference)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_total_decomp <- plot_ly(
  data = total_growth_by_category,
  x = ~category_short,
  y = ~growth_spending_trillions,
  type = "bar",

  marker = list(color = total_gradient),

  text = ~label,
  textposition = "outside",
  textfont = list(
    size = 12,
    color = "#065f46"
  ),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "Total spending growth: <b>$%{y:.2f}T</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Where Total Healthcare Spending Growth Accumulates<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Allocation of total spending growth across healthcare services (last decade)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Service Category",
      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Additional Spending (Trillions USD)",
      tickprefix = "$",
      ticksuffix = "T",
      range = c(0, y_max_fixed),   
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    showlegend = FALSE,
    cliponaxis = FALSE,

    margin = list(
      l = 80,
      r = 40,
      t = 130,
      b = 100
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_total_decomp
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Total spending growth (10-year window)
# --------------------------------------------------
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)
start_year  <- latest_year - 10

total_growth_contrib <- table01_tidy %>%
  filter(year %in% c(start_year, latest_year)) %>%
  summarise(
    total_growth_pct =
      (last(total_spending) / first(total_spending) - 1),
    base_spending =
      first(total_spending)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    total_growth_spending = base_spending * total_growth_pct
  ) %>%
  pull(total_growth_spending)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Recent category spending shares
# --------------------------------------------------
category_shares <- category_ts %>%
  filter(year >= latest_year - 3) %>%     # recent mix
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_spending = mean(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share = avg_spending / sum(avg_spending)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Allocate total growth across categories
# --------------------------------------------------
total_growth_by_category <- category_shares %>%
  mutate(
    growth_spending_trillions = share * total_growth_contrib / 1000
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(growth_spending_trillions)) %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category),
    category_short = recode(
      category,
      "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
      "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
      "Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
      "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
      "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
      "Dental Services" = "Dental",
      "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
      "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
    ),
    label = paste0("$", sprintf("%.2f", growth_spending_trillions), "T")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Gradient bar colors (lighter green)
# --------------------------------------------------
total_gradient <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#9ae6c1", "#059669")
)(
  nrow(total_growth_by_category)
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_total_decomp <- plot_ly(
  data = total_growth_by_category,
  x = ~category_short,
  y = ~growth_spending_trillions,
  type = "bar",

  text = ~label,
  textposition = "outside",
  textfont = list(
    size = 12,
    color = "#065f46"
  ),

  marker = list(color = total_gradient),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "Total spending growth: <b>$%{y:.2f}T</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Where Total Healthcare Spending Growth Accumulates<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Allocation of total spending growth across healthcare services (last decade)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Service Category",
      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
  title = "Additional Spending (Trillions USD)",
  range = c(0, 0.6),   # tight + meaningful ceiling
  tickvals = c(0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6),
  ticktext = c("$0", "$0.2T", "$0.4T", "$0.6T"),
  gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
  zeroline = FALSE
)
,

    showlegend = FALSE,
    cliponaxis = FALSE,

    margin = list(
      l = 80,
      r = 40,
      t = 100,   # space for labels above bars
      b = 100
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_total_decomp
```

### Per Capital Spending Growth 

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Per-capita-driven spending growth (10-year window)
# --------------------------------------------------
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)
start_year  <- latest_year - 10

per_capita_growth_contrib <- table01_tidy %>%
  filter(year %in% c(start_year, latest_year)) %>%
  summarise(
    per_capita_growth_pct =
      (last(spending_per_capita) / first(spending_per_capita) - 1),

    base_spending =
      first(total_spending)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Total spending added purely due to per-capita growth
    per_capita_growth_spending = base_spending * per_capita_growth_pct
  ) %>%
  pull(per_capita_growth_spending)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Average recent category spending shares
# --------------------------------------------------
category_shares <- category_ts %>%
  filter(year >= latest_year - 3) %>%   # recent mix
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_spending = mean(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share = avg_spending / sum(avg_spending)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Allocate per-capita-driven growth by category
# --------------------------------------------------
per_capita_growth_by_category <- category_shares %>%
  mutate(
    growth_spending = share * per_capita_growth_contrib,
    growth_spending_trillions = growth_spending / 1000   # billions → trillions
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(growth_spending_trillions)) %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category),
    category_short = recode(
      category,
      "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
      "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
      "Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
      "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
      "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
      "Dental Services" = "Dental",
      "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
      "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Add labels 
# --------------------------------------------------
per_capita_growth_by_category <- per_capita_growth_by_category %>%
  arrange(growth_spending_trillions) %>%  
  mutate(
    label = paste0("$", sprintf("%.2f", growth_spending_trillions), "T")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Gradient: light → dark (higher values darker)
# --------------------------------------------------
pc_gradient <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#fde68a", "#f59e0b")   
)(
  nrow(per_capita_growth_by_category)
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Tight y-axis padding (≈ 8%)
# --------------------------------------------------
y_max <- max(per_capita_growth_by_category$growth_spending_trillions, na.rm = TRUE)
y_max_padded <- y_max * 0.5   

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_per_capita_decomp <- plot_ly(
  data = per_capita_growth_by_category,
  x = ~category_short,
  y = ~growth_spending_trillions,
  type = "bar",

  marker = list(color = pc_gradient),

  text = ~label,
  textposition = "outside",
  textfont = list(
    size = 12,
    color = "#92400e"  
  ),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "Per-capita cost growth: <b>$%{y:.2f}T</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Where Per‑Capita Cost Growth Adds Healthcare Spending<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Allocation of per‑person cost growth across healthcare services (last decade)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Service Category",
      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Additional Spending (Trillions USD)",
      tickprefix = "$",
      ticksuffix = "T",
      range = c(0, y_max_padded),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    showlegend = FALSE,
    cliponaxis = FALSE,

    margin = list(
      l = 80,
      r = 40,
      t = 100,   
      b = 100
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_per_capita_decomp

```

### Population Growth

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Population-driven spending growth (10-year window)
# --------------------------------------------------
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)
start_year <- latest_year - 10

pop_growth_contrib <- table01_tidy %>%
  filter(year %in% c(start_year, latest_year)) %>%
  summarise(
    population_growth_pct =
      (last(population) / first(population) - 1),

    base_spending =
      first(total_spending)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Total spending added purely due to population growth
    population_growth_spending = base_spending * population_growth_pct
  ) %>%
  pull(population_growth_spending)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Average category spending shares (recent years)
# --------------------------------------------------
category_shares <- category_ts %>%
  filter(year >= latest_year - 3) %>%   # recent mix
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(
    avg_spending = mean(spending, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share = avg_spending / sum(avg_spending)
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Allocate population-driven growth by category
# --------------------------------------------------
population_growth_by_category <- category_shares %>%
  mutate(
    # pop_growth_contrib is already in dollars (billions if total_spending is)
    growth_spending_billions = share * pop_growth_contrib
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(growth_spending_billions)) %>%
  mutate(
    category = factor(category, levels = category),
    category_short = recode(
      category,
      "Hospital Care" = "Hospitals",
      "Physician and Clinical Services" = "Physicians",
      "Prescription Drugs" = "Rx Drugs",
      "Nursing Care Facilities and Continuing Care Retirement Communities" = "Nursing Care",
      "Home Health Care" = "Home Health",
      "Dental Services" = "Dental",
      "Durable Medical Equipment" = "Medical Equipment",
      "Other Non-Durable Medical Products" = "Other Medical"
    )
  )

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Prep for plotting (BILLIONS)
# --------------------------------------------------
population_growth_plot <- population_growth_by_category %>%
  arrange(growth_spending_billions) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("$", sprintf("%.1f", growth_spending_billions), "B")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Gradient: light → dark blue
# --------------------------------------------------
pop_gradient <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#bfdbfe", "#1d4ed8")
)(
  nrow(population_growth_plot)
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Fixed y-axis max
# --------------------------------------------------
y_max_fixed <- 50   # or 50 if you prefer

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_population_decomp <- plot_ly(
  data = population_growth_plot,
  x = ~category_short,
  y = ~growth_spending_billions,
  type = "bar",

  marker = list(color = pop_gradient),

  text = ~label,
  textposition = "outside",
  textfont = list(
    size = 12,
    color = "#1e3a8a"
  ),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "Population-driven growth: <b>$%{y:.1f}B</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Where Population Growth Adds Healthcare Spending<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Allocation of population-driven cost growth across healthcare services (last decade)",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Healthcare Service Category",
      showgrid = FALSE
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Additional Spending (Billions USD)",
      tickprefix = "$",
      ticksuffix = "B",
      range = c(0, y_max_fixed),
      tickvals = seq(0, y_max_fixed, by = 10),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    showlegend = FALSE,

    margin = list(
      l = 90,
      r = 40,
      t = 100,
      b = 100
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_population_decomp

```


Column {data-width="50%"}
-------------------------------------

### Whats Driving Helthcare Spending Growth?

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Ensure growth_decomp exists (knit-safe)
# --------------------------------------------------
latest_year <- max(table01_tidy$year, na.rm = TRUE)
start_year  <- latest_year - 10

growth_decomp <- table01_tidy %>%
  filter(year %in% c(start_year, latest_year)) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  summarise(
    population_growth =
      (last(population) / first(population) - 1),
    per_capita_growth =
      (last(spending_per_capita) / first(spending_per_capita) - 1),
    total_spending_growth =
      (last(total_spending) / first(total_spending) - 1),
    base_spending =
      first(total_spending)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(population_growth, per_capita_growth, total_spending_growth),
    names_to = "driver",      
    values_to = "growth_pct"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    growth_pct = growth_pct * 100,
    growth_amount_trillions = base_spending * (growth_pct / 100) / 1000,

    driver = recode(
      driver,
      population_growth     = "Population Growth",
      per_capita_growth     = "Per‑Capita Spending Growth",
      total_spending_growth = "Total Spending Growth"
    ),

    driver = factor(
      driver,
      levels = c(
        "Total Spending Growth",
        "Per‑Capita Spending Growth",
        "Population Growth"
      )
    ),

    label = paste0(sprintf("%.1f", growth_pct), "%")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Order + gradient prep
# --------------------------------------------------
growth_decomp_plot <- growth_decomp %>%
  arrange(growth_pct)

growth_gradient <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#bfdbfe", "#1d4ed8")
)(
  nrow(growth_decomp_plot)
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Fixed y-axis max
# --------------------------------------------------
y_max_fixed <- 85

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_growth_decomp <- plot_ly(
  data = growth_decomp_plot,
  x = ~driver,
  y = ~growth_pct,
  type = "bar",

  marker = list(color = growth_gradient),

  text = ~label,
  textposition = "outside",
  textfont = list(size = 12, color = "#1e3a8a"),

  hovertemplate = paste(
    "<b>%{x}</b><br>",
    "Growth: <b>%{y:.1f}%</b><br>",
    "Amount: <b>$%{customdata:.2f}T</b>",
    "<extra></extra>"
  ),

  customdata = ~growth_amount_trillions
) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "What’s Driving Healthcare Spending Growth?<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Ten‑year growth decomposition of U.S. healthcare spending across healthcare services",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.94,
      font = list(size = 22)
    ),

    xaxis = list(title = NULL, showgrid = FALSE),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Growth Over Last 10 Years (%)",
      ticksuffix = "%",
      range = c(0, y_max_fixed),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    showlegend = FALSE,

    margin = list(l = 80, r = 40, t = 110, b = 100),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_growth_decomp
```


### Per Capita Vs GDP Per Capita


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(plotly)
library(scales)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Prepare per-capita vs GDP per-capita data (KNIT-SAFE)
# --------------------------------------------------
pc_vs_gdp <- table01_tidy %>%
  mutate(
    gdp_per_capita = (gdp * 1e9) / (population * 1e6)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(spending_per_capita), !is.na(gdp_per_capita)) %>%
  arrange(year)

# Index both series to first year = 100
base_health <- first(pc_vs_gdp$spending_per_capita)
base_gdp    <- first(pc_vs_gdp$gdp_per_capita)

pc_vs_gdp <- pc_vs_gdp %>%
  mutate(
    health_index = 100 * spending_per_capita / base_health,
    gdp_index    = 100 * gdp_per_capita / base_gdp,
    date = as.Date(paste0(year, "-01-01"))
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Tight y-axis limits (fills blank vertical space)
# --------------------------------------------------
y_min <- 95
y_max <- max(pc_vs_gdp$health_index, pc_vs_gdp$gdp_index, na.rm = TRUE)
y_max_padded <- y_max * 1.05

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------
p_pc_vs_gdp <- plot_ly() %>%

  add_trace(
    data = pc_vs_gdp,
    x = ~date,
    y = ~health_index,
    type = "scatter",
    mode = "lines",
    name = "Healthcare Spending per Capita",
    line = list(color = "#dc2626", width = 3.5),
    hovertemplate = paste(
      "<b>%{x|%Y}</b><br>",
      "Healthcare spending index: <b>%{y:,.0f}</b>",
      "<extra></extra>"
    )
  ) %>%

  add_trace(
    data = pc_vs_gdp,
    x = ~date,
    y = ~gdp_index,
    type = "scatter",
    mode = "lines",
    name = "GDP per Capita",
    line = list(color = "#2563eb", width = 3.5),
    hovertemplate = paste(
      "<b>%{x|%Y}</b><br>",
      "GDP per capita index: <b>%{y:,.0f}</b>",
      "<extra></extra>"
    )
  ) %>%

  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "Healthcare Costs vs Income Growth<br>",
        "<span style='font-size:13px; color:#6b7280;'>",
        "Indexed growth of healthcare spending per person compared with GDP per capita",
        "</span>"
      ),
      x = 0.5,
      y = 0.95,
      font = list(size = 22, color = "#111827")
    ),

    xaxis = list(
      title = "Year",
      title_standoff = 12,  
      tickformat = "%Y",
      showgrid = FALSE,
      rangeselector = list(
        buttons = list(
          list(count = 10, label = "10Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(count = 20, label = "20Y", step = "year", stepmode = "backward"),
          list(step = "all", label = "All Time")
        )
      ),
      rangeslider = list(
        visible = TRUE,
        thickness = 0.04,
        bgcolor = "#e5e7eb"
      )
    ),

    yaxis = list(
      title = "Amount (Thousands USD)",
      range = c(y_min, y_max_padded),
      gridcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.06)",
      zeroline = FALSE
    ),

    legend = list(
      orientation = "h",
      x = 0.5,
      y = -0.18,
      xanchor = "center"
    ),

    hovermode = "x unified",

    margin = list(
      l = 80,
      r = 40,
      t = 100,
      b = 90   
    ),

    plot_bgcolor  = "#ffffff",
    paper_bgcolor = "#ffffff",
    font = list(
      family = "Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif",
      size = 12
    )
  ) %>%
  config(displaylogo = FALSE)

p_pc_vs_gdp
```
